events.name: Brighton & Hove Albion at Aston Villa

headlines.description: A hat-trick from forward Ollie Watkins fired Aston Villa to a sensational 6-1 thrashing of high-flying Brighton in the Premier League on Saturday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Watkins scores hat trick as Villa rout Brighton

headlines.description: Arsenal maintained their unbeaten start to the season with a 4-0 win against Bournemouth on Saturday as Kai Havertz opened his account for the club.
headlines.shortLinkText: Havertz scores as Arsenal thrash Bournemouth

headlines.description: Two goals from set pieces earned Luton Town their first ever Premier League victory as they beat Everton 2-1 at Goodison Park on Saturday to climb out of the bottom three and pile on misery on the hosts.
headlines.shortLinkText: Luton sink Everton to claim first Prem victory

headlines.description: Manchester United slumped to their fourth defeat of the Premier League season as an early Joachim Anderson strike earned Crystal Palace a 1-0 win at Old Trafford on Saturday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Man Utd woes continue in 1-0 defeat to Palace

headlines.description: Miguel Almiron and Alexander Isak both struck as Newcastle beat Burnley 2-0 in the Premier League on Saturday to claim a third straight win in all competitions.
headlines.shortLinkText: Newcastle sweep aside winless Burnley at home

events.name: Sheffield United at West Ham United

headlines.description: Jarrod Bowen and Tomas Soucek's first-half goals secured a comfortable 2-0 home Premier League victory for West Ham over Sheffield United on Saturday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Bowen, Soucek score as West Ham ease past Sheffield United

headlines.description: Hwang Hee-chan scored the winner as Wolves ended Manchester City's perfect start to the Premier League season with a shock 2-1 home win on Saturday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Wolves end City's perfect PL start with shock win

headlines.description: Tottenham Hotspur left it late to down nine-man Liverpool 2-1 and had an own goal by Joel Matip to thank for their stoppage-time win as the defender turned the ball home to leave Spurs second in the Premier League behind Manchester City on Saturday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Spurs beat nine-man Liverpool thanks to late OG
